texto: The objections to religion are of two sorts -- intellectual and moral. The intellectual objection is that there is no reason to suppose any religion true
autor: the moral objection is that religious precepts date from a time when men were more cruel than they are and therefore tend to perpetuate inhumanities which the moral conscience of the age would otherwise outgrow.
explicacao: Citação de the moral objection is that religious precepts date from a time when men were more cruel than they are and therefore tend to perpetuate inhumanities which the moral conscience of the age would otherwise outgrow. sobre temas ligados a reflexao. “The objections to religion are of two sorts -- intellectual and moral. The intellectual objection…” articula reflexao, bertrand-russell, religion em registro de reflexao, com relevância para contextos como reflexao.
